rag_rules: Place generated AGREE annex blocks in the target component or implementation scope, following AADL scoping rules
Preserve existing AADL architecture by only adding annex blocks, not modifying other elements
Use visible identifiers from the current context, avoiding implementation-only paths in component type annexes
Place input assumptions and output guarantees in component type annexes when using concrete bounds or visible relations
Use AGREE logical operators and nested if-then-else for branching logic in contracts
generic_rules: Maintain AADL architecture integrity by only adding annex blocks, not modifying other elements
Use visible identifiers from the current context, avoiding implementation-only paths in component type annexes
Place input assumptions and output guarantees in component type annexes when using concrete bounds or visible relations
Use AGREE logical operators and nested if-then-else for branching logic in contracts
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};`
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations, e.g., `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`
AGREE scalar types are int, real, and bool
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
Do not use AGREE scalar type names like integer or boolean; use int, real, and bool instead
